website_note: Site active and confirmed as Centerstone Health Services, an Indiana FQHC Look-Alike nonprofit. Note: separate from Centerstone (centerstone.org), the larger national behavioral health org.
description: Centerstone Health Services is a nonprofit Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC) Look-Alike in Indiana providing accessible primary care, behavioral health, addiction recovery, and pediatric services to underserved communities, with clinics in Connersville, Bloomington, Columbus, and Richmond, IN.
recent_news: Centerstone Health Services expanded to Columbus and Richmond, IN, using state and federal opioid settlement funding. In January 2026, SAMHSA restored $14.3M in federal grants to Centerstone programs across multiple states including Indiana. CCBHC Improvement and Advancement Grant active through 2026.
verification_notes: Original record had no company name or state — both inferred from website URL. Casey Brewster's title is listed as 'Vice President'; ZoomInfo indicates Board Member role. Also holds position at Scott County Community Foundation. This company (centerstonehealthservices.org) is distinct from Centerstone (centerstone.org).
enrichment_notes: Original record missing company name and state. Founded 2019 per first clinic in Connersville. FQHC Look-Alike designation confirmed. Limited public exec data for this smaller nonprofit affiliate.

website_note: dynojet.com is the confirmed official website. Company is formally headquartered in Las Vegas, NV; manufacturing facility is in Belgrade, MT. The batch entry state of MT likely reflects the Belgrade manufacturing site.
description: Dynojet Research is a world leader in development and manufacturing of performance enhancement products for powersports vehicles, including chassis dynamometers, Power Commander fuel management systems, Power Vision ECU tuners, clutch kits, and ignition products for motorcycles, ATVs, UTVs, and snowmobiles.
recent_news: In January 2022, CARB settled with Dynojet Research and Dynatek Ignitions for $2.1M over emissions violations for non-exempted aftermarket parts sold in California. Previously acquired by Irving Place Capital and New Value Capital in November 2015; Radial Equity Partners also listed as owner. No major news specific to 2024-2025.
verification_notes: Multiple data discrepancies: (1) State listed as MT but HQ is Las Vegas, NV; MT is the manufacturing location. (2) Founded listed as 2007 in original — incorrect; all sources confirm 1972. (3) Employees listed as 7 — incorrect; LinkedIn and other sources indicate ~92 employees. (4) Industry listed as 'Holding Companies' — inaccurate; company manufactures powersports products. (5) Shawn Arnold listed as 'Executive' but not found in current public leadership data.
enrichment_notes: Original record appears to reflect a holding company entity rather than the operating company. Dynojet Research is a well-established manufacturer. Company website active and product range well-documented. Founded 1972 verified from company's own About Us page.

website_note: CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: gallo.com is the website for E&J Gallo Winery, a major California wine company — this is NOT the Gallo-Schwegmann Partnership. The listed website is incorrect for this Louisiana building finishing company.
description: Gallo-Schwegmann Partnership is a small Louisiana-based building finishing partnership with very limited public data. The Gallo name in Louisiana construction is associated with the Gallo family (Gallo Mechanical LLC), and the Schwegmann name with the New Orleans grocery family. The specific operations of this partnership as an entity could not be independently confirmed.
recent_news: No recent news found for this specific entity. The broader Gallo Mechanical LLC was acquired by a Dallas private equity firm (reported ca. 2020-2023). Schwegmann grocery chain closed in 1997.
verification_notes: Website (gallo.com) is E&J Gallo Winery — clearly incorrect for a small Louisiana building partnership. August Gallo Jr. is confirmed on LinkedIn as Chairman of the Board at Gallo Mechanical LLC (a separate/related entity). The 'Gallo-Schwegmann Partnership' as a distinct entity was not found in public business databases under that exact name.
enrichment_notes: Very limited public data. Website discrepancy is a critical data quality issue. Small, privately-held partnership with minimal online presence. Recommend manual verification of correct website URL and company details. The listed website (gallo.com) belongs to an entirely different and unrelated company.

recent_news: On September 12, 2024, Atlantic, Tomorrow's Office acquired InfoAxis Inc. — the 15th acquisition for Atlantic. Mickey McGraw's team was noted for organic growth and client-centered service philosophy. In July 2025, Atlantic announced executive succession with Jason Weiss as CEO and Adam Weiss as President, with Larry Weiss moving to Chairman.

Scott Hallam, President and COO, succeeded him as CEO and joined the Board of Directors. Steven Tramonte joined in 2024 to lead commercial operations. Boardwalk has been named a Best Places to Work in Kentucky for 2 consecutive years. Parent company operates ~14,310 miles of pipeline across the US.

website_note: cu-portland.edu remains live as a closure information page only. The institution permanently ceased operations after the Spring 2020 semester. The campus was purchased by the University of Oregon in June 2022. This is no longer an active institution.
description: Concordia University Portland was a private, Lutheran Church-Missouri Synod liberal arts university in Portland, Oregon, founded in 1905. It closed in July 2020 following mounting financial challenges and the withdrawal of support from its parent organization, the Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod. At closure, it had approximately 5,000 students and 1,500 employees. The campus is now UO Portland.

contacts.name: Michael Greenlee
contacts.title: Former Library Director, Concordia University School of Law (school closed 2015; Greenlee now at Notre Dame Law School)

recent_news: Concordia University Portland voted to close February 7, 2020; final day of instruction July 11, 2020. In June 2022, the University of Oregon finalized purchase of the campus for The Ballmer Institute for Children's Behavioral Health. Michael Greenlee, the listed contact, left before closure: he became Associate Law Librarian at University of Idaho College of Law, and then joined Notre Dame Law School in July 2023 as Associate Director of Kresge Law Library.
verification_notes: Multiple critical discrepancies: (1) Institution is CLOSED (since 2020) — this lead is defunct. (2) State listed as ID in original record but institution was in Portland, OR. (3) Michael Greenlee was library director at Concordia School of Law which closed in 2015, 5 years before the broader university closure. He is now Associate Director at Notre Dame Law School. This entire lead record is outdated and the entity no longer exists.
enrichment_notes: DEFUNCT LEAD — institution closed July 2020. Should be removed from active prospecting. State in original record (ID) is incorrect; institution was in Portland, OR. Michael Greenlee is no longer affiliated with any Concordia entity; currently at Notre Dame Law School (as of July 2023). Original data likely pre-2015 vintage.
